remember: Students will be able to recall the definition of Alternating Current (AC) and Direct Current (DC).
Students will be able to list the advantages of Alternating Current over Direct Current.
Students will be able to identify the different types of AC circuits: series, parallel, and series-parallel.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of frequency and its relationship with the number of cycles per second.
Students will be able to describe the process of electromagnetic induction and how it leads to AC generation.
Students will be able to understand the principle of AC generator and its construction.
apply: Students will be able to calculate the rms value of AC voltage and current from the peak values.
Students will be able to determine the impedance of an AC circuit and its relation with resistance and reactance.
Students will be able to solve problems involving series and parallel AC circuits.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the advantages and disadvantages of AC over DC for power transmission.
Students will be able to compare the performance of AC and DC motors.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of AC in modern power systems.

ncert_objectives: To study the principles of Alternating Current and its applications.
To understand the advantages of Alternating Current over Direct Current.
To analyze the different types of AC circuits and their behavior.
board_exam_objectives: Calculate the rms value of AC voltage and current from the peak values.
Determine the impedance of an AC circuit and its relation with resistance and reactance.
Solve problems involving series and parallel AC circuits.
